mnemonics.title: Remember Separator Types
mnemonics.content: P-B-B-S-C-D-AC: Parentheses, Braces, Brackets, Semicolon, Comma, Dot, Arrow, Colon. Think: 'Please Be Brave, Stay Calm, Don't Act Crazy' - P(arens) B(races) B(rackets) S(emicolon) C(omma) D(ot) A(rrow) C(olon)

acronyms.expansion: Brackets, Commas, Semicolons - the three essential separators
acronyms.usage: Remember BCS for basic code structure separators in Java

acronyms.term: P B B S C D AA
acronyms.expansion: Parentheses, Braces, Brackets, Semicolons, Commas, Dots, Arrows, Apostrophes (for chars)

visualTips.visualization: Type[] name - brackets near type; name[index] - brackets near name. Think: 'Declaration attaches to type, access attaches to variable'
method() uses empty curved parentheses; array[index] uses square brackets with number. Visualize () as 'call' (sound) and [] as 'index' (number inside)
-> points from parameters to body, like an arrow showing direction of data flow. Left of arrow = input, right of arrow = output

analogies.analogy: Just as a period ends a sentence in English, a semicolon ends a statement in Java. Without it, sentences run together and become confusing.
Curly braces {} are like containers or rooms in a house - they define spaces where related code activities happen together.
Just as periods separate parts of an address (house number, street, city), the period separates package parts and accesses class members.
Parentheses around method parameters are like hands cupped together holding the data being passed to the method.
